title: Formal obstruction recursion
summary: Recursively solves the formal equations Lₛ=0 for normal jets Fₛ₊₁ and defines a normalized formal obstruction expression P₂ℓ, without interpreting it as a geometric differential operator.

mathematicalContent.heading: Solving normal jets
mathematicalContent.body: At step s, the equation Lₛ=0 is solved formally for Fₛ₊₁ by multiplying the rest term by the negative inverse of its highest-jet coefficient and substituting previously solved jets.

mathematicalContent.heading: Obstruction coefficient
mathematicalContent.body: For ℓ>0 the obstruction is the coefficient equation L_{ℓ−1} after replacing every normal jet by its recursive formal solution.

mathematicalContent.heading: Normalization
mathematicalContent.body: The formal P₂ℓ expression multiplies the obstruction by aℓ⁻¹=(-1)^ℓ2^{2ℓ−2}((ℓ−1)!)². Lean checks the first three scalar values.

correspondence.leanType: def substF (σ : ℕ → FormalExpr) : FormalExpr → FormalExpr
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Recursively substitutes a chosen expression σ(m) for each normal-jet symbol Fₘ while preserving all other syntax constructors.
Defines the scalar used to solve Lₛ=0 for Fₛ₊₁.
Recursively defines F₀=f and solves each higher jet using previously solved lower jets.
Exposes the auxiliary recursive solution as the formal solved jet.

correspondence.leanType: theorem solvedJet_zero (ell : ℕ) : solvedJet ell 0 = FormalExpr.f
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves the zeroth solved jet is the boundary datum f.
Defines the substitution that replaces every Fₘ by its solved expression.
Defines the formal obstruction coefficient; it is zero at ℓ=0 and substituted L_{ℓ−1} for positive ℓ.
Defines the inverse normalization factor for positive ℓ.
Defines the normalized formal obstruction expression called P₂ℓ.

references.locator: Formal Juhl-type formula and OR bridge
references.note: Primary source for the intended curved OR–Juhl identity.
Source for the Juhl side of the formal syntax.

limitations: This is a formal recursive syntax calculation. It does not prove that the highest coefficient is invertible in a geometric setting, interpret Dⱼ or trⱼ, establish equality with canonical GJMS operators, or justify the obstruction equation analytically.
